run.true_intent: Exactly that: a weekday 9am standup that lists yesterday's merged PRs from our main repo and posts the summary to our #standup Slack channel. The only thing I didn't say is WHICH repo and WHICH Slack channel.
run.vague_intent: Every weekday at 9am, collect yesterday's merged pull requests from our repo and post a standup summary to Slack.

transcript.question: Should the digest be posted to Slack automatically, or do you want to approve it first?
options: A: Auto-post — publish every morning without intervention
B: Always wait — show me the draft and I'll approve before it goes live
C: On low confidence — auto-post unless something looks off (missing PRs, fetch errors)
transcript.answer: C — auto-post unless something looks off, then check with me first.

transcript.question: How should "Slack Standup Poster" format its output? Paste / attach an example, or pick a shape:
options: Markdown — bullet list with short headings
Markdown — table or grid layout
Prose summary — short paragraphs, no bullets
JSON — structured payload
I'll attach an example
transcript.answer: Markdown — bullet list with short headings, one bullet per merged PR.

transcript.question: What should "Daily PR Collector" remember between runs? (Pick "Nothing" if it doesn't need memory.)
options: User preferences and corrections
Items I've already approved or rejected
Recurring context (people, projects, topics I care about)
Nothing — each run is independent
transcript.answer: Nothing — each run is independent, it should just pull yesterday's merged PRs fresh every morning.

capabilities.title: Daily PR Collector
capabilities.description: Fetches all PRs merged into the configured repo since yesterday 9am and formats a bullet-list standup digest.

subscriptions.description: Emitted when the PR digest is formatted and ready for the Slack poster to publish

capabilities.title: Slack Standup Poster
capabilities.description: Publishes the formatted PR bullet list to the configured Slack channel, with a confidence gate before auto-posting.

subscriptions.description: Receives the formatted digest payload from uc_daily_pr_collector
Emitted after a successful Slack post for downstream coordination

trigger.description: Fires whenever uc_daily_pr_collector emits a ready digest

run.true_intent: Give me a short summary of the biggest AI and startup news twice a week — Monday and Thursday mornings — pulled from the web, delivered as an in-app notification. Just reading, nothing to publish.
run.vague_intent: keep me updated on tech news

transcript.question: How do you want to stay updated on tech news?
options: A: Scheduled digest — collect headlines daily (or weekly) and deliver a curated summary on a cadence you choose
B: Real-time monitor — watch for breaking stories and alert you the moment something significant lands
C: Interactive advisor — ask me about tech news on demand and I'll research and brief you instantly
transcript.answer: A — a digest twice a week, Monday and Thursday mornings.

transcript.question: What should "Bi-Weekly Tech Digest" remember between runs? (Pick "Nothing" if it doesn't need memory.)
options: User preferences and corrections
Items I've already approved or rejected
Recurring context (people, projects, topics I care about)
Nothing — each run is independent
transcript.answer: Recurring context — the AI and startup topics and companies I care about, so it doesn't keep repeating the same stories.

transcript.question: Should "Bi-Weekly Tech Digest" wait for your approval before publishing its output?
options: Never — auto-publish; I can undo/discard myself
On low confidence — only pause when unsure
Always — I want to sign off every run
transcript.answer: Never — auto-publish, it's just a summary I read.

transcript.question: Which storage connector should "Bi-Weekly Tech Digest" use?
transcript.answer: No storage — just send it to me as an in-app notification.

transcript.question: How should "Bi-Weekly Tech Digest" fire?
options: A: On demand — I'll trigger it manually
B: On a schedule (daily/weekly/…)
C: When an external event occurs (e.g. new document, inbound message)
transcript.answer: B — on a schedule, twice a week: Monday and Thursday mornings.

capabilities.title: Bi-Weekly Tech Digest
capabilities.description: Searches the web for notable tech stories twice a week, avoids repeating previously covered stories, and auto-delivers a curated ranked digest as an in-app notification every Monday and Thursday morning.

subscriptions.description: Emitted after each digest is delivered, carrying story count and the top headline.

trigger.description: Every Monday and Thursday at 8 am Eastern

run.true_intent: Whenever we publish a new product launch page, I want a short summary posted to our team's Notion 'Launches' database so everyone can see it. The trigger is a new launch being published; the destination is Notion, not chat.

options: Always — I want to review and approve every announcement before it goes out
Never — auto-post once the draft looks good

transcript.answer: Never — auto-post once the draft looks good. I don't want to be a bottleneck on every launch summary.

run.true_intent: When I add or complete a task in Todoist, mirror that change into a Notion 'Tasks' database so my Notion workspace always reflects Todoist. It's one-way, Todoist to Notion — Notion is just the readable mirror.
run.vague_intent: sync my tasks between my two tools

transcript.question: Which two task tools should be synced, and how should the sync flow?
options: A: ClickUp ↔ Linear — two-way real-time sync (changes in either propagate instantly)
B: Asana ↔ Linear — two-way real-time sync
C: ClickUp ↔ Asana — two-way real-time sync
D: One tool is the master, the other mirrors it on a schedule (pick tools + direction next)
E: Different pair — tell me which two
transcript.answer: E: Todoist and Notion — Todoist is the source of truth and Notion just mirrors it, one-way.

transcript.question: What should "Todoist → Notion Mirror Sync" remember between runs? (Pick "Nothing" if it doesn't need memory.)
options: User preferences and corrections
Items I've already approved or rejected
Recurring context (people, projects, topics I care about)
Nothing — each run is independent
transcript.answer: Nothing — each run is independent. It just needs to push whatever changed in Todoist over to Notion.

transcript.question: Should "Todoist → Notion Mirror Sync" wait for your approval before publishing its output?
options: Never — auto-publish; I can undo/discard myself
On low confidence — only pause when unsure
Always — I want to sign off every run
transcript.answer: Never — auto-publish. It's just mirroring my Todoist into Notion, so I don't need to approve every sync.

transcript.question: Which storage connector should "Todoist → Notion Mirror Sync" use?
transcript.answer: Notion — that's where the mirrored Tasks database lives.

transcript.question: How should "Todoist → Notion Mirror Sync" fire?
options: A: On demand — I'll trigger it manually
B: On a schedule (daily/weekly/…)
C: When an external event occurs (e.g. new document, inbound message)
transcript.answer: C — when something changes in Todoist (a task added or completed), it should fire then.

capabilities.title: Todoist → Notion Mirror Sync
capabilities.description: Polls Todoist every 5 minutes for new or changed tasks and upserts them into the user's Notion Tasks database. Fires only when changes are detected.

subscriptions.description: Emitted after each poll run where changes were detected and processed, with counts of tasks created, updated, completed, and errors.

trigger.description: Poll Todoist every 5 minutes — approximates event-driven firing since Todoist has no native push connector
